sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Torino have struggled recently, with only 1 win in their last 5 matches, including heavy defeats like a 6-0 loss to Como. Lecce are in even worse shape, winless in their last 7 games and failing to score in their last 3 outings. Both teams are in poor form, but Torino might have a slight edge playing at home.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Lecce have had the upper hand, winning both matches (2-1 and 1-0). Historically, their games tend to be low-scoring, with under 2.5 goals in 7 of their last 8 meetings. This suggests a tight contest is likely again.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Torino, sitting 16th with 23 points, have conceded 40 goals this season (18th in the league), showing defensive frailties. Lecce, 17th with 18 points, have struggled offensively with only 13 goals scored, the second-lowest in the league. Both teams lack consistency in key areas, but Torino's slightly better attacking output could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Torino’s Nikola Vlašić (5 goals, 2 assists) could be a threat, while Lecce miss key midfielder Medon Berisha (2 goals, 3 assists), impacting their creativity. Torino also have absences like Giovanni Simeone, but their squad depth might still give them an advantage. Lecce’s defensive solidity will be tested without key contributors.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sofascore’s prediction (44% home win, 31% draw, 25% away win) and Torino’s home advantage, they are slight favorites despite recent struggles. Lecce’s inability to score and poor away form further tilt the balance. Expect a low-scoring game with Torino likely edging it.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Torino leads Lecce by a single goal, with the scoreline reading 1-0. The home side capitalized on an early opportunity through Che Adams, who struck in the 29th minute. Torino has shown a stronger attacking intent in patches, while Lecce has struggled to convert their possession into meaningful chances. Both teams have had their moments, but Torino's clinical finishing has made the difference so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Torino's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Torino started the game with purpose, dominating the early exchanges and creating a big chance that eventually led to their goal. Their ability to penetrate the final third and enter the penalty area has been notable, and they’ve shown efficiency in front of goal. Defensively, they’ve held a strong line, winning a significant portion of duels and aerial battles. Che Adams has been pivotal, not only scoring but also contributing to the build-up play, while Nikola Vlašić has been instrumental in creating opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Lecce's first-half performance
halftimeSections.text: Lecce, on the other hand, has had more possession overall but lacked the cutting edge in the final third. Despite frequent entries into dangerous areas and a considerable number of corners, they’ve failed to create any big chances. Their duel-winning rate has been lower, and they’ve struggled to assert dominance in physical battles. Players like Riccardo Sottil have shown glimpses of creativity, but the team as a whole needs to find a way to unlock Torino’s defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw significant shifts between the two sides. Torino began with a sustained period of dominance, controlling the attacking play for a large portion of the opening stages. Their pressure paid off with consistent attacking spells leading up to the goal. However, Lecce responded with a strong surge in the middle of the half, mounting a series of dangerous moves that briefly put Torino on the back foot. Towards the end of the half, momentum oscillated, with Lecce pushing hard for an equalizer while Torino looked to protect their lead.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: For Torino, maintaining their defensive solidity while looking to exploit counter-attacking opportunities will be crucial. Lecce needs to capitalize on their possession and turn their final third entries into clear-cut chances. Set pieces, particularly Lecce’s corners, could play a big role if they can improve their delivery and finishing. Discipline will also be key, as Torino has already picked up a yellow card, which could lead to vulnerabilities if Lecce presses aggressively.
